$grounding: Grounded in: docs/design/ui-dual-tone-tokens.json (WEB tone tokens — this surface lives in apps/web), apps/web/app/courses/[courseId]/items/[itemId]/page.tsx (the canonical learner course-player: AppShell + ghost back Button + PageHeader with Badge(type)+Chip(status) carried by TEXT + a .ci-grid two-column layout — reading column + sticky 'In this module' aside — Card body + an info Alert for 'coming soon' + a disabled placeholder action; all visuals resolve from var(--lms-*) tenant tokens), apps/web/app/lib/branding.ts pattern (getBranding(tenantId), tenant accent via --lms-accent), and handshake feat-scorm-import-play.md §4.F routes (GET /scorm/packages/:id launch info; PUT /scorm/packages/:id/runtime save cmi; GET .../runtime?learnerId read-back), §4.E cmi->normalized status mapping, §6 follow-ups (full JS RTE bridge window.API / API_1484_11 and byte-serving package assets are documented follow-ups; learnerId currently from body), and §4.G (terminal/passing attempts emit LEARNING_EVENT_CAPTURED so results reach the gradebook/analytics path). ALL tokens REUSED from the WEB tone; NO new palette. Net-new patterns (sandboxed launch iframe + an explicit 'mark complete' control standing in for the full RTE bridge) are flagged in openQuestions; everything else reuses the existing course-player chrome and @lms/ui components.
goal: A learner launches a SCORM package, works through it, and has their completion + score recorded against them — and can see their current attempt status at any time. Because the full SCORM JS runtime bridge is a documented follow-up, the learner can also explicitly report completion via the runtime endpoint (a server-side record), so progress is never stuck.
route: /courses/[courseId]/scorm/[packageId]  (learner app; reached from the course/module item list the way /courses/[courseId]/items/[itemId] is)
informationHierarchy: The content itself: the launch surface (iframe of launchHref under blobUrl, or a clearly-labelled placeholder while asset-serving is the follow-up) — the learner is here to do the activity
Their current status & score: completion (completed/incomplete/not started), pass/fail, and score — what counts toward their record
The action that advances them: 'Mark complete' / 'Report result' (calls the runtime endpoint) with clear, announced feedback
Orientation: package title + course context + a 'Back to course' link
Honest scope note: the package's runtime assets and the automatic JS RTE bridge are coming soon — meanwhile completion is recorded by the explicit action
wiresTo: On load: GET /scorm/packages/:id (-> { package: { id, version, title, launchHref, masteryScore, blobUrl } }) for launch info + GET /scorm/packages/:id/runtime?learnerId=<session.userId> (-> { attempt | null }) for current status. Launch URL = blobUrl joined with the relative launchHref (when asset-serving is live). On 'Mark complete'/'Report result': PUT /scorm/packages/:id/runtime { learnerId, completionStatus, successStatus?, scoreScaled?, scoreRaw?, lessonStatus?, sessionTime? } -> 200 { attempt: { completionStatus, successStatus, scoreScaled, scoreRaw, ... } }. A terminal/passing attempt server-side emits LEARNING_EVENT_CAPTURED (results reach analytics/gradebook).
states: asset-serving-follow-up (launchHref can't be served yet -> placeholder panel)

designTokens.$note: WEB tone from docs/design/ui-dual-tone-tokens.json, exposed at runtime as --lms-* vars via ThemeStyle tone='web' (roomier, softer, body targets AAA). accent is the TENANT accent via --lms-accent. Reference vars, never literals.

color.surface2: var(--lms-surface-2) /* #F2F1EC — iframe frame + status rail fill */

typography.fontFamily: var(--lms-font-family) /* tenant font, else system-ui fallback */

space.$map: --lms-space-1..8; reuse the course-player .ci-* rhythm (grid gap space-5, body gap space-4/3)

regions: main (back Button -> PageHeader -> .sc-grid: launch column + status/completion aside)

composition.desktop: Reuse the course-player .ci-grid model: a two-column CSS grid minmax(0,1fr) + ~20rem. Left = the launch column (LaunchSurface iframe/placeholder, full width of its column, 16:9-ish min-height, fluid). Right = a sticky CompletionPanel aside (current status, score, and the report/mark-complete action).
composition.tablet: Single column; CompletionPanel moves BELOW the launch surface (still a full Card).
composition.phone: Single column, stacked: launch surface first (min-height ~52vh, fluid, never wider than viewport), CompletionPanel below. No horizontal overflow at 360px; the iframe is width:100% with a fluid aspect-ratio box.

breakpoints.approach: mobile-first, fluid. The launch surface is a responsive aspect-ratio container (width:100%) so the iframe scales without horizontal overflow; on phone it gets a generous min-height and the learner scrolls the page, not a clipped frame. Status text uses overflow-wrap:anywhere. Nothing overflows at 360px.

components.name: BackLink (reuse Button variant=ghost size=sm, like .ci back link)
PageHeader (reuse @lms/ui — mirror course-player header)
components.purpose: Title + course context + the at-a-glance status, exactly like the content-item player.
components.content: title 'Workplace Safety Fundamentals' · subtitle '{course.code} - SCORM module' · Badge 'SCORM 1.2' · Chip status (see StatusChip).

a11y.label: title is the h1; Badge/Chip carry TEXT not color alone

components.name: LaunchSurface (NET-NEW — sandboxed iframe OR documented placeholder)
components.purpose: Host the SCORM content. When asset-serving is live, embed launchHref resolved under blobUrl in a titled, sandboxed iframe. Until then, render an honest placeholder so the learner is never staring at a broken frame.
anatomy: A bordered frame (1px var(--lms-border-strong), radius var(--lms-radius-md), bg var(--lms-surface-2)) wrapping a responsive aspect-ratio box

LIVE variant: <iframe title='SCORM content: {package title}' src='{resolved launch URL}' sandbox='allow-scripts allow-same-origin' allow='fullscreen' loading='lazy' width=100% height=100%> + an 'Open in full screen' / 'Open in a new tab' affordance above the frame

PLACEHOLDER variant: centered content — an inline-SVG content/play glyph (aria-hidden) + heading 'Launch coming soon' + body explaining asset-serving is a follow-up + the launch href shown read-only (monospace) so it's transparent what WOULD load
components.content: Placeholder body: 'This SCORM package's interactive content will play here once package files are served. Its launch page is {launchHref}. In the meantime, use the panel to record your completion.' Live frame caption (sr + visible): 'Interactive SCORM content. If it doesn't load, use Open in a new tab.'
states: loading (skeleton block in the aspect-ratio box)
load-error (frame failed -> inline Alert tone=warning 'Couldn't load the content here. Open it in a new tab.' + a link)

responsive.phone: full-width, min-height ~52vh, aspect-ratio fluid; page scrolls
responsive.desktop: fills the left grid column, ~16:9, generous height

components.interactions: iframe is sandboxed (allow-scripts allow-same-origin only; NO allow-top-navigation / allow-popups-to-escape-sandbox) so embedded content can't break out or hijack the page. 'Open in new tab' is a normal link with rel='noopener noreferrer'. The frame must be reachable but must NOT create a keyboard trap — Tab moves into and back out of the iframe normally; an 'Open in a new tab' control gives a guaranteed escape.

a11y.role: iframe MUST have a descriptive title attribute ('SCORM content: {title}'); placeholder is a labelled region with a heading
a11y.label: iframe title names the content; placeholder heading + body convey state in text
a11y.keyboard: no keyboard trap — focus enters and leaves the iframe with Tab; provide a visible focusable 'Open in a new tab' escape; if a learner can't operate embedded content, the CompletionPanel still lets them record progress

components.name: StatusChip (reuse Chip @lms/ui — same STATUS_META text+tone pattern as the course-player)
components.purpose: Convey completion + pass/fail by TEXT and tone, never color alone (matches items/[itemId] STATUS_META).
anatomy: Chip with a text label; an aria-hidden status dot may accompany, like .ci-rail__dot
components.content: Completion: 'Not started' (tone neutral) / 'In progress' (tone accent) / 'Completed' (tone success). When a success_status exists, append it: 'Completed - Passed' (success) or 'Completed - Failed' (danger). 'Not started' when attempt is null or not_attempted.

responsive.phone: in PageHeader actions, wraps under title

components.interactions: static; reflects the latest attempt (re-renders after a successful PUT)

a11y.label: meaning in the Chip TEXT; color is reinforcement only (WCAG noColorOnly)

components.name: CompletionPanel (reuse Card + Stack + Chip + Button; sticky aside on desktop)
components.purpose: Show the learner's current attempt and let them record/report completion via the runtime endpoint.
anatomy: Card (sticky aside on desktop, full-width Card below on tablet/phone) with h2 'Your progress'

A small definition list: Status (StatusChip), Result (Passed/Failed/Not yet determined), Score (e.g. '82% (0.82)' or 'No score recorded'), Mastery (the package masteryScore as a target, e.g. 'Passing score 80%' or 'No passing score set'), Last updated (relative time of attempt.updatedAt)
An action region with the report/mark-complete control(s) + an aria-live status
A short honest note: 'Your result is recorded against your account and reported to your instructor.'
components.content: Empty (no attempt): Status 'Not started', Result 'Not yet determined', Score 'No score recorded'. After completion: Status 'Completed - Passed', Result 'Passed', Score '82% (0.82)', 'Passing score 80%', 'Updated just now'.
variants: follow-up (no auto-RTE -> the explicit action is the primary path)
states: completed-locked (optional: once completed, action becomes 'Mark in progress again' OR stays as 'Update result' — see openQuestions)

responsive.phone: full-width Card below the launch surface; dl stacks
responsive.desktop: sticky aside ~20rem beside the launch column (top: var(--lms-space-5))

components.interactions: The MarkCompleteControl PUTs the runtime endpoint and the panel re-renders from the returned attempt; the StatusChip and score update in place; the aria-live region announces the result.

a11y.role: complementary (aside) with an h2; <dl> for the status pairs; aria-live=polite status region for save feedback
a11y.label: h2 'Your progress' names the region; values carried by text
a11y.keyboard: controls in logical order after the launch surface; Enter/Space activate

components.name: MarkCompleteControl (reuse Button @lms/ui + optional small score Field)
components.purpose: The explicit, honest stand-in for the full JS RTE bridge: let the learner record completion (and optionally a score) server-side via PUT /scorm/packages/:id/runtime.
anatomy: Primary Button 'Mark complete' (sends completionStatus='completed'); for graded packages an optional disclosure 'Report a score' reveals a small numeric Field (0-100) -> sent as scoreRaw with scoreMax=100 so the backend computes scoreScaled, and successStatus derived against masteryScore
aria-live=polite status line under the button
components.content: Button 'Mark complete'. Disclosure 'Report a score (optional)'. Field label 'Your score (out of 100)'. Status: 'Saving your progress...', 'Progress saved.', 'Couldn't save - try again.' Helper: 'Use this to record that you've finished. Automatic tracking from the content itself is coming soon.'
states: busy (Button aria-busy + disabled to prevent double-submit)
error (Alert tone=danger role=alert + retry; entered score retained)

responsive.desktop: Button auto width within the aside

components.interactions: Click -> PUT { learnerId: session.userId, completionStatus:'completed', scoreRaw?, scoreMax: 100? } -> on 200 update panel from returned attempt; on a passing/terminal attempt the backend emits LEARNING_EVENT_CAPTURED (no client work). learnerId is taken from the session today (see openQuestions re: server-trusted identity).

a11y.role: button + optional labelled numeric input + aria-live status
a11y.label: Button names the action; score Field has a <label>; result announced politely; errors via role=alert
a11y.keyboard: Enter/Space activate; disclosure toggles with Enter/Space (aria-expanded); numeric input standard entry

components.name: FollowUpNote (reuse Alert tone=info/warning — mirror the course-player 'coming soon' info Alert)
components.purpose: Be honest that automatic SCORM tracking + asset-serving are follow-ups, so the explicit Mark-complete path makes sense to the learner.
anatomy: Alert (tone=info) with the scope note; placed near the launch surface or top of the CompletionPanel
components.content: 'This SCORM module records your completion when you mark it complete. Automatic progress and score tracking directly from the content is coming soon.'

a11y.label: meaning in text + inline-SVG info icon, never color alone

components.name: NotFound / ServiceOffline (reuse Alert @lms/ui + PageHeader, like the player notFound path)
anatomy: Alert tone=warning with real copy + a back link
components.content: Package 404: 'This SCORM module no longer exists or was moved.' · Service offline: 'The content service is unavailable. Start it (CONTENT_STORE=memory pnpm dev in services/content) to play this module.'
components.interactions: terminal with a 'Back to course' link

a11y.label: text + inline-SVG warning icon, never color alone

flows: Happy path (ungraded): open the module -> LaunchSurface shows the content (or placeholder while asset-serving is a follow-up) + CompletionPanel shows 'Not started' -> learner works through it -> 'Mark complete' -> 'Saving your progress...' -> PUT 200 -> StatusChip flips to 'Completed', 'Progress saved.' announced; backend emits LEARNING_EVENT_CAPTURED.

Happy path (graded): masteryScore present -> learner opens 'Report a score', enters 82 -> Mark complete -> backend computes scaled 0.82 >= 0.80 -> attempt returns success_status passed -> StatusChip 'Completed - Passed', Score '82% (0.82)'.
Returning learner: GET runtime returns an existing attempt -> panel pre-populated with their status/score on load (no re-entry needed).
Not started: attempt null -> StatusChip 'Not started'; Mark complete available.
Loading: skeleton in the launch box + skeleton dl rows while package + attempt fetch.
Asset-serving follow-up: launchHref can't be served -> LaunchSurface placeholder explains it and shows the launch href; completion still recordable.
Report error: PUT fails -> Alert tone=danger role=alert + retry; entered score retained; status unchanged.
Package not found / service offline: PageHeader 'Module unavailable' + warning Alert + back link; no attempt fetched.
Reduced motion: skeleton shimmer and any frame/chip transitions respect prefers-reduced-motion.

accessibility.contrastMin: 4.5:1 body text / 3:1 large text, UI & focus ring (web tone verified: text 15.2:1, muted 8.3:1, focus #1E4E8C >=7:1)
accessibility.focusOrder: logical DOM order — back link -> PageHeader -> 'Open in a new tab' (launch escape) -> iframe content -> CompletionPanel (status -> score disclosure -> Mark complete). Visible :focus-visible 3px var(--lms-focus) ring offset 2px on every interactive element.
accessibility.iframe: the launch <iframe> MUST have a descriptive title attribute ('SCORM content: {package title}'); it is sandboxed (allow-scripts allow-same-origin; no top-navigation/popup-escape); it must NOT trap keyboard focus — Tab enters and exits normally, and a visible focusable 'Open in a new tab' control guarantees an escape and an alternative if embedded content is inoperable.
accessibility.status: completion/pass/fail conveyed by Chip TEXT + tone, never color alone (matches the course-player STATUS_META); save feedback in an aria-live=polite region; errors via role=alert; the StatusChip update after a PUT is announced.
accessibility.noKeyboardTrap: explicitly tested — focus must be able to leave the iframe; the 'Open in a new tab' link and the CompletionPanel are always keyboard-reachable.
accessibility.motion: respect prefers-reduced-motion — disable skeleton shimmer and transitions; keep instant color state changes and a static focus ring.
accessibility.semantics: AppShell header/main landmarks; single h1 (package title) -> h2 'Your progress'; CompletionPanel is a <complementary> aside; status pairs in a <dl>; iframe titled; score input labelled via Field; aria only where native won't do.
accessibility.touchTargets: 44x44px minimum for back link, open-in-new-tab, score disclosure, Mark complete, and retry.
accessibility.noColorOnly: every status, result, and error carries a text label (+ inline-SVG icon for alerts) in addition to tone.

openQuestions: FULL RTE BRIDGE (documented follow-up, per handshake §6): the SCORM JS runtime bridge (window.API for 1.2 / window.API_1484_11 for 2004 that the content reads/writes) is NOT built for #31. This surface records completion via the EXPLICIT MarkCompleteControl -> PUT /scorm/packages/:id/runtime. Confirm wording so learners understand auto-tracking is coming; when the bridge lands, the explicit control can become a fallback/manual override.

ASSET SERVING (documented follow-up, §6): byte-serving the package's runtime files under blobUrl is not built. Confirm whether to render the live sandboxed iframe (when a served launch URL is available) OR ship the placeholder variant first. Design supports BOTH via the LaunchSurface variants; recommend placeholder-first, iframe behind a feature check on whether the launch URL resolves.

LEARNER IDENTITY (handshake §6 UNKNOWN): the runtime routes take learnerId from the body today; the content service has no x-user-id resolver. Design sends session.userId from the web app via a BFF route (mirror app/api/uploads) that forwards the authenticated user. Confirm the gateway/identity plan so a learner can't report progress as someone else (server-trusted identity). security-agent gate.

NEW BFF ROUTES: this surface needs web BFF routes to GET /scorm/packages/:id, GET .../runtime, and PUT .../runtime (forwarding x-tenant-id + the authenticated learnerId). Confirm placement alongside the existing web lib/dashboard fetch helpers. frontend-dev builds; security-agent reviews.

RE-OPEN AFTER COMPLETE: should a learner be able to change a 'Completed' attempt back (re-attempt)? Current backend = single upserted attempt (UNIQUE tenant+package+learner); multiple historical attempts are a follow-up (§6). Design defaults to 'Update result' staying available (idempotent upsert). Confirm whether to lock after completion.
SCORE INPUT FOR UNGRADED PACKAGES: only show 'Report a score' when masteryScore is present (graded). For ungraded packages offer completion-only. Confirm this gating with product.

GRADEBOOK SURFACING (§4.G): results reach analytics/LRS via LEARNING_EVENT_CAPTURED today; a dedicated grading-side consumer is a follow-up. The panel says 'reported to your instructor' — confirm that's accurate for the current path or soften to 'recorded against your account'.
